title: Advanced Modeling Capstone and Reproducible Research · Mastery Check
summary: Build, test, and communicate a model involving an independently reviewed applied research question. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a reproducible capstone reproducibility computational study. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Formulate research question in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive computational experiment in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute verification validation in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Research Question evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Research Question assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Formulate research question in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the boundaries of the idea, compare a valid case with a near miss, and trace the causal difference.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: math advanced capstone reproducibility. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Research Question, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a reproducible capstone reproducibility computational study.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: research question can be applied from a memorized formula without checking assumptions, conditioning, or validation. This fails the Research Question contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 22 units, changes by −9 units, then by +8 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Reproducible Release applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Communicate reproducible release in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

hints: Name the Reproducible Release precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Research Question to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Research Question.

checks.description: The decision applies Computational Experiment to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Computational Experiment.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Verification Validation.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Uncertainty Communication.

checks.description: The decision applies Reproducible Release to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Reproducible Release.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.
checks.failureMessage: Recalculate from the documented model, check the scale, and include the requested unit.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using communicate and reproducible plus specific case evidence.
